fields.description: F3: Buffer Penetration % boundaries for Green → Yellow → Red → Black zones.
fields.label: Zone Thresholds

fields.description: F3: BP% ≥ this value = Red Zone (produce/order IMMEDIATELY). Default: 67%
fields.label: Red Zone Threshold (%)

fields.description: F3: BP% ≥ this value = Yellow Zone (plan replenishment). Default: 33%
fields.label: Yellow Zone Threshold (%)

fields.description: When checked, the daily scheduler automatically creates Material Requests for Red/Yellow items.
fields.label: Auto-Generate Material Requests

fields.description: Which buffer zones should trigger automatic Material Request creation.
fields.label: Generate MR for Zones
fields.options:
Red and Black Only
Red, Black, and Yellow

fields.description: After creating Work Orders from a Production Plan, automatically walk the full multi-level BOM tree, check each component's Bin.actual_qty, and create Purchase Material Requests for items with shortages where 'Auto Purchase TOC' is enabled on the Item. Applies the Min Purchase / Production Qty floor from the Item's Min Order Qty child table: order_qty = max(shortage, min_order_qty).
fields.label: Auto-Create Component Purchase MRs After WO Creation

fields.description: Users who receive automated TOC Engine alerts: component shortage MR summaries (after each WO generation) and daily min-order-qty configuration warnings (12 AM).
fields.label: TOC Engine Notifications

fields.description: Add users to receive TOC Engine operational emails. Per-user flags control which alert types they receive.
fields.label: TOC Engine Notification Users

fields.description: Send email notification when items enter Red or Black zone.
fields.label: Email Alert on Red Zone

fields.description: Newline-separated role names. Users with these roles receive Red zone email alerts.
fields.label: Alert Recipient Roles

fields.description: F7/F8: Auto-adjustment of buffer sizes based on observed behaviour.
fields.label: Dynamic Buffer Management (DBM)

fields.description: When checked, buffers auto-adjust weekly via TMR (increase) and TMG (decrease).
fields.label: Enable DBM Auto-Adjustment

fields.description: F7 TMR Trigger: If stock in Red > this % of RLT in one cycle → increase buffer. Default: 20%
fields.label: TMR: Red % of RLT

fields.description: F8 TMG Trigger: Stock must be in Green for this many full RLT cycles → decrease buffer. Default: 3
fields.label: TMG: Green Cycles Required

fields.description: F7/F8: TMR increases by +this%, TMG decreases by −this%. Default: 33%
fields.label: DBM Adjustment %

fields.description: Safeguard: Max consecutive TMR increases before requiring manual review. Prevents runaway inflation.
fields.label: Max Consecutive TMR Increases

fields.description: Safeguard: TMG will never shrink a buffer below this value. Prevents buffer collapse.
fields.label: Minimum Buffer Floor

fields.description: Default values used when configuring new buffer rules.
fields.label: Calculation Defaults

fields.description: F1: Default VF for new buffer rules. 1.0-1.3=stable, 1.3-1.6=moderate, 1.6-2.0=volatile.
fields.label: Default Variability Factor

fields.description: F1: Number of days to look back when auto-calculating Average Daily Usage. Default: 90 days.
fields.label: ADU Lookback Days

fields.description: F6: Demand Adjustment Factor. 1.0=normal. Diwali=1.6. Summer=0.7. Applied to ALL buffers.
fields.label: Current Global DAF

fields.description: Name of the active seasonal event (e.g. 'Diwali 2026', 'Summer Slowdown'). Informational only.
fields.label: Current DAF Event

fields.description: CONFIGURE FIRST before enabling items. Defines which warehouses count as on-hand stock, WIP, or are excluded entirely (Scrap, Expiry, Wastage).
fields.label: Inventory Classification

fields.description: Classify every warehouse in your company. Inventory = usable on-hand stock. WIP = in-process (adds to WIP in F2). Excluded = never counted (Scrap, Expiry, Wastage, QC Hold, Demo). Leave empty to use the warehouse defined on each item's buffer rule (backward-compatible).
fields.label: Warehouse Classification

fields.description: DeepSeek API key for the AI Advisor tab in WO Kitting Planner. Get a key at https://platform.deepseek.com/api_keys
fields.label: AI Advisor (DeepSeek)

fields.description: Starts with sk-. Stored encrypted. Used by wo_kitting_planner AI Advisor tab. Fallback: site_config.json deepseek_api_key key.
fields.label: DeepSeek API Key

fields.description:
📋 Production Automation Guide

Each month your sales team submits a Sales Projection. This section allows the system to automatically generate Work Orders to fulfill those specific production targets.

▶ How it Works
  1. Schedule: Every day at 06:00 AM, the system checks for a SUBMITTED Sales Projection for the current month.
  2. Calculation: For each item, the system calculates demand using the following logic:
    Net Required = (Projected Qty + Carryover Pending SOs) − Current Month Pending SOs − (Current Stock + Open WO Qty)
    • Carryover Pending SOs: Unfulfilled orders due before this month.
    • Open WO Qty: Quantity in open/draft orders (prevents double-ordering).
  3. Execution: If Net Required > 0 and Production Capacity is set, a Draft Work Order is created.
  4. Recursive BOM: Sub-assemblies are automatically triggered for the full BOM tree.
⚠️ Capacity Gate
If Capacity is 0 or blank, no Work Order is created. You will be notified via email.
⚠️ BOM Gate
If no active Default BOM is found, the system skips the item and sends an email alert.
Manual Trigger: Use the 'Run Projection Automation' button on any submitted Projection.
✓ Safe by Design: The system accounts for existing orders; running it twice will not create duplicates.
fields.label: Sales Projection Automation

fields.description:
⚙️ Automation Switch

Control how the system handles monthly Sales Projections. Toggle this setting to enable or disable background production triggers.

ON
System auto-creates Draft Work Orders daily at 06:00 AM for the current month's submitted Sales Projection.
OFF
Sales Projections are reference-only. No automatic Work Orders will be generated.
Manual Trigger: The 'Run Projection Automation' button is restricted by this setting. If set to OFF, manual runs are also disabled.
fields.label: Enable Projection Automation

fields.default:
To Deliver and Bill
To Deliver
On Hold
fields.description:
📊 Pending Demand Filter

Define which Submitted ($docstatus=1$) Sales Order statuses should be treated as active demand in the production formula.

INCLUDE
Statuses that represent unfulfilled items.
Typical: To Deliver and Bill, To Deliver, On Hold.
EXCLUDE
Do NOT include: Completed, Closed, or To Bill. These represent items that have already been physically delivered.
Formula Impact: These values are additive defaults used to calculate Carryover Pending SOs and Current Month Pending SOs.
fields.label: Pending Sales Order Statuses — Submitted SOs (one per line)

fields.description:
🔗 Draft Demand Logic

Define specific Workflow States for Draft Sales Orders ($docstatus=0$) that should be treated as committed demand, bypassing the standard status list above.

DRAFT ONLY
Counted as Committed Demand regardless of the main statuses.
Standard Default: Confirmed
Custom Workflows: If your internal Sales Order approval process uses different state names (e.g., "Validated", "Pre-Approved"), update this list to ensure accurate demand tracking.
fields.label: Confirmed Draft SO Workflow States (one per line)

fields.description:
📧 Email Notification Settings

Add users to the table below to subscribe them to automated alerts. Each user can receive three types of updates:

ON EDIT
Triggered every time a Sales Projection draft is saved.
ON SUBMIT
Triggered when a Sales Projection is officially finalized/submitted.
ON WO RUN
Full summary report (daily or manual) including:
  • List of Work Orders created
  • Items skipped and specific reasons (Capacity/BOM)
fields.label: Notification Users

fields.description: Create/delete test data to verify all TOC triggers work correctly.
fields.label: Demo Data (Admin Only)

fields.description: JSON list of all demo doc IDs created. Used for cleanup.
fields.label: Demo Data Manifest
